1 3 2 
biodiversidadProtección y promoción de la14.
Corredores biológicos 
Áreas de Alto Valor de Conservación 
Programas de reforestación 
Manuelita en la COP-16 


Estamos comprometidos con la sostenibilidad 
ambiental y la conservación de la 
biodiversidad en todas nuestras operaciones.Corredores
biológicos
1 3 3 Manuelita Azúcar y Energía  protege corredores 
biológicos y cuencas hidrográﬁcas en el Valle del Cauca, 
mediante la reforestación, mantenimiento y protección del 
ecosistema. 
Durante el 2023 y 2024, realizó la siembra de 2.695  árboles 
en las cuencas de los ríos cercanos, alcanzando 7.862 
árboles sembrados en 14,3  kilómetros protegidos.CañaManuelita Azúcar y Energía recibió una subvención 
del fondo Bonsucro Impact Fund  para la restauración 
de ecosistemas naturales y mejoramiento del ﬂujo de 
especies nativas del Valle del Cauca.
Este es un proyecto de articulación y colaboración 
interinstitucional en alianza con Cenicaña, RG y CIA, 
Asoamaime y Asobolo.  Su propósito se centra en 
cuatro objetivos principales:
Establecimiento de corredores 
de conectividad ecológica para 
fomentar el ﬂujo de 
biodiversidad.
Construcción y fortalecimiento 
de viveros para la propagación 
y recuperación de especies 
nativas en cuencas de los ríos 
Amaime y Bolo.Implementación 
de una red de 
monitoreo 
hidrológico.
Uso eﬁciente de 
los recursos 
hídricos en riego.


En sus operaciones de Palma en el Meta y 
Casanare, Manuelita conserva y protege un total 
de 1.256 hectáreas denominadas como Áreas de 
Alto Valor de Conservación (AVC), en las cuales 
se prohíben actividades extractivas, cultivos, 
caza y pesca.
En 2024, Manuelita Aceites y Energía  
llevó a cabo jornadas de sensibilización para 
colaboradores y comunidades del área de 
inﬂuencia e implementó monitoreo de 
especies raras, amenazadas o en peligro en 
las 848 hectáreas de AVC  que protege. 
Por su parte, Palmar de Altamira 
implementa un plan de conservación para 
407,9 hectáreas de AVC , promoviendo la 
recuperación de la vegetación y los suelos, 
así como la sensibilización de colaboradores 
y comunidades cercanas a la plantación. Palma
1 3 4 Áreas de Alto Valor
de Conservación 


1 3 5 Programas
de reforestación  
En 2023 Manuelita Azúcar y 
Energía inauguró su Vivero 
Forestal La Rita, con una capacidad 
de producción de 38.000 plántulas 
nativas para la reforestación del 
bosque seco tropical. En 2024 se 
desarrollaron jornadas de 
recolección de semilla y 
germinación, con una producción 
de 23.000  plántulas. 
En 2023 Agroindustrial Laredo  
implementó su programa de 
reforestación para fortalecer la 
protección de la biodiversidad en 
sus campos de cultivo, iniciando 
con la siembra de 1.430 árboles en 
las zonas de Laredo y Arena Dulce.
En 2024, continuó con la siembra 
de 540 árboles más distribuidos en 
distintas áreas de cultivo.Caña Palma
A 2024, Manuelita Aceites 
y Energía  ha realizado la 
siembra de  65.780  plantas 
en 59,8  hectáreas destinadas 
para reforestación.  
A su vez, en 2024, Palmar de 
Altamira   realizó la siembra 
de 2.689  árboles en 136,6 
hectáreas de reforestación 
que limitan con las Áreas de 
Alto Valor de Conservación. Ver video


En el marco de COP-16 realizada en Cali, Colombia 
en octubre de 2024, Manuelita dio a conocer sus 
avances en materia de sostenibilidad ambiental 
en la agroindustria , con un enfoque en la 
economía circular y la integración de la 
biodiversidad en su modelo de negocio.Esta iniciativa fue una oportunidad 
para interactuar con la comunidad y 
compartir el proyecto de huertas 
comunitarias.Participación en 4 paneles dirigidos a diferentes 
grupos de interés.
Participación en stand en la zona verde con 
talleres experienciales de huertas urbanas y 
caseras, en colaboración con la Universidad ICESI.
1 3 6 Manuelita
en la COP-16
Ver más


1 3 7 
sueloCuidado del 15.
Producción y aplicación de compost  
y residuos orgánicos


1 3 8 En nuestros campos utilizamos fertilizantes orgánicos  y empleamos 
sistemas de riego que protegen el suelo .
También adoptamos tecnologías que previenen la comp actación 
de suelos , que limita la circulación del aire y del agua, y tiene efectos 
negativos sobre la capacidad productiva de los cultivos.Producción y aplicación
de compost  y residuos
orgánicos Manuelita Azúcar y Energía y Agroindustrial 
Laredo producen compost a partir de residuos 
orgánicos de sus procesos productivos.Caña
PalmaEntre 2023 y 2024 Manuelita Azúcar y 
Energía aplicó compost en 1.250  hectáreas
de cañas orgánicas.
Entre 2023 y 2024, Agroindustrial Laredo  
incrementó los contenidos de materia orgánica 
en los suelos desérticos de Arena Dulce, Pur 
Pur y campos orgánicos de Laredo, con la 
aplicación de compost en 1.373  hectáreas.
Al cierre de 2024, Manuelita Aceites y 
Energía  aplicó compost en 1.300  hectáreas 
de cultivo, reemplazando el uso de 
fertilizantes químicos en 270,7  hectáreas.
Palmar de Altamira  realiza la aplicación de 
raquis, subproducto de la extracción de 
aceite de palma, para el abono de 796 
hectáreas de cultivo.Ver video


Economía
circular
16.
Transformación de residuos
Créditos de carbono
Aprovechamiento y disposición ﬁnaly gestión de residuos
1 3 9 


En Manuelita aprovechamos los residuos que 
resultan de nuestros procesos agroindustriales , 
en nuestros procesos de economía circular.Transformación
de residuos
1 4 0 Biomasa para la producción de energía
El bagazo de caña, la ﬁbra, el cuesco, y 
raquis de palma , son un tipo de biomasa que 
se obtiene como residuo de la molienda de 
caña de azúcar y la extracción de aceite de 
palma, que utilizamos para la producción de 
energía eléctrica .  
La biomasa tiene varias ventajas ambientales, 
como la reducción de gases de efecto 
invernadero , y la disminución de la 
dependencia de los combustibles fósiles.
Eﬂuentes industriales para Fertirriego
Aplicamos los eﬂuentes industriales , 
generados en la extracción de aceite crudo 
de palma, como fertirriego para el mismo 
cultivo, eliminando los vertimientos de 
aguas  residuales a los cuerpos de agua 
superﬁciales y reduciendo el uso de 
fertilizantes químicos.


1 4 1 Producción de pellets a partir
de bagazo
Agroindustrial Laredo  desarrolla pellets que 
se emplean en procesos de calefacción, 
generación de energía y alimentación animal. 
Por su origen natural es un producto ecológico 
y sostenible que reemplaza el uso de 
combustibles fósiles como el carbón.Residuos orgánicos en Compost
Producimos compost a partir de 
residuos orgánicos de nuestros 
procesos productivos como la cachaza, la 
ceniza, entre otros, para la fertilización 
orgánica de cultivos.
Fardos para la producción de
compost y energía eléctrica renovable
Agroindustrial Laredo  realiza la 
compactación de biomasa en fardos que se 
utilizan para la producción de compost y 
generación de energía eléctrica renovable.


1 4 2 Cada crédito de carbono otorgado es equivalente a
una tonelada de CO² que se deja de emitir a la atmósfera.Comprometidos con la reducción del impacto ambiental  
de nuestras operaciones, generamos productos de valor 
agregado a partir de nuestro enfoque de economía circular . 
Apalancados en este modelo de negocio, hemos alcanzado 
créditos de carbono , que comercializamos en los mercados 
voluntarios de compensación de emisionesManuelita Azúcar y Energía 
alcanzó en 2024 la certiﬁcación 
de 170.308 créditos de 
carbono, otorgados para el 
periodo 2018-2021, por sus 
iniciativas para la reducción de 
emisiones de CO2.Manuelita Aceites y Energía  
alcanzó en 2023 la certiﬁcación 
de 189.301 créditos de 
carbono, otorgados para el 
periodo 2021-2022, por sus 
iniciativas para la reducción de 
emisiones de CO2.Créditos de carbono

# Mejillones


Ofrecemos mejillones congelados en distintos formatos y empaques para nuestros distintos clientes y mercados: Carne de Mejillón IQF, Mejillón Media Concha IQF y Mejillón Entero en su jugo, y una línea de mejillón entero con salsas. Nuestros mejillones son producidos en condiciones ambientales destacadas con alta calidad y consistencia, a costo competitivo, lo que nos permite estar presentes en más de 20 mercados y abastecer distintos canales de venta: sector industrial y de procesamiento, foodservice y retail.

# 398 historias que hoy cultivan cosas buenas


![398 historias que hoy cultivan cosas buenas](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/398-historias-que-hoy-cultivan-cosas-buenas.jpg)


En Manuelita celebramos la graduación de 398 personas que culminaron su bachillerato académico en 2025, entre colaboradores de menores ingresos y miembros de comunidades cercanas a nuestras operaciones en Colombia y Perú. Este logro representa mucho más que un título: es el resultado del esfuerzo, la disciplina y las ganas de salir adelante.


A través de nuestros programas sociales, acompañamos a adultos y jóvenes en extraedad en su proceso educativo, brindándoles herramientas que les permiten fortalecer sus capacidades y acceder a mejores oportunidades laborales, educativas y de desarrollo personal.


Creemos en el poder de la educación como motor de transformación. Por eso, impulsamos iniciativas que no solo abren puertas, sino que también construyen caminos de progreso para las personas y sus familias.


Cada graduación es una muestra de lo que se puede lograr cuando el compromiso individual se encuentra con el acompañamiento adecuado. En Manuelita apoyamos estos procesos que transforman vidas y fortalecen comunidades.


Así cultivamos cosas buenas.


[![Compartir en Facebook](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_facebook_white.svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-noticias/398-historias-que-hoy-cultivan-cosas-buenas/)  [![Compartir en Twitter](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_twitter_white.svg)](http://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-noticias/398-historias-que-hoy-cultivan-cosas-buenas/&text= 398 historias que hoy cultivan cosas buenas)

# Informe de Sostenibilidad 2023-2024


La sostenibilidad ha sido la esencia de nuestra gestión empresarial por más de 160 años. Se fundamenta en nuestro legado y en nuestro propósito central de generar progreso y bienestar con empresas y productos ejemplares, a partir del aprovechamiento racional y sostenible de los recursos naturales.


[Ver informe](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Informe-de-Sostenibilidad-Manuelita-2023-2024.pdf)


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Foto-presidente.png) ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Foto-presidente-responsive.png)


## Mensaje del presidente


“Nuestro séptimo Informe de Sostenibilidad destaca los logros más importantes en sostenibilidad económica, social, ambiental y buen gobierno corporativo entre 2023 y 2024. Reﬂeja nuestro progreso en 16 prioridades de sostenibilidad, y reaﬁrma nuestro compromiso de crear valor económico y social, fortaleciendo nuestras iniciativas para el cuidado del planeta”


![Gobierno Corporativo](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/gobierno-corporativo-card.png)


#### Gobierno Corporativo


Basamos nuestra gestión en una tradición centenaria de integridad, con altos estándares de ética, transparencia y fiel...






![Ambiental](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/ambiental-card.png)


#### Ambiental


Estamos comprometidos con el cuidado del medio ambiente y la conservación de los ecosistemas de las regiones...


![Social](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/social-card.png)


#### Social


La gestión de nuestro talento humano es un proceso estratégico que busca atraer, desarrollar y fidelizar a...


![Económico](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/economico-card.png)


#### Económico


A través de la gestión efectiva de un portafolio de empresas que entregan productos diferenciados con valor...


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/sostenibilidad.png) ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/sostenibilidad-responsive.png)


## Prioridades de Sostenibilidad


Manuelita establece 16 prioridades de acción a través de un modelo de sostenibilidad que enmarca su estrategia corporativa y competitiva de sus unidades de negocio, en los ámbitos económico, social, ambiental, y de buen gobierno corporativo.  
   
  
 Estas prioridades también declaran su compromiso de contribuir al cierre de brechas en los Objetivos de Desarrollo Sostenible establecidos por las Naciones Unidas para el año 2030.


[Conoce las prioridades aquí](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Informe-Manuelita-gobierno-corporativo.pdf)

# Frutas y hortalizas


Manuelita ofrece uva de mesa de alta calidad, en variedades flame, sugraone, crimson y red globe, a los importadores más exigentes, principalmente en Estados Unidos, Europa, y Asia. Nuestras uvas se producen en Ica, al sur del Perú, región que cuenta con ventajas comparativas por condiciones privilegiadas agro climáticas y de contra estación con los productores del hemisferio norte.


**Producido en Perú por Manuelita Frutas y Hortalizas.**


![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/manuelita_horizontal_01.png)


![Flame](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Flame.jpg)


Flame ![Flame](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


 ![Sugraone](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Sugraone.jpg)


Sugraone ![Sugraone](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


 ![Red Globe](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/RedGlobe.jpg)


Red Globe ![Red Globe](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


 ![Crimson](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/crimson-seedless.jpg)


Crimson ![Crimson](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


La flame seedles es una variedad de uva de mesa, sin semilla, de bayas de color rojo y pulpa jugosa. Su sabor es dulce, ligeramente aromático. Ideal para consumir fresca o en combinación con quesos.  
   
 La uva flame se cosecha y comercializa desde la última semana de octubre hasta finales de diciembre.   
   
 Comercializamos la variedad flame a mercados en Europa y Estados Unidos, con nuestras marcas **Manuelita e Inka Grapes**.  
   
 ![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Uvamesa_04.jpg)  
   
 **Disponibles en presentaciones:**  


  
* Caja de 4.5 Kg - Incluye 6 bolsas por caja
  
* Caja de 5 Kg - Incluye 10 Clamshells de 0.5 Kg por caja, o 10 Punnets de 500 g por caja
  
* Caja de 7.3 Kg - Incluye 8 Clamshells de 2Lb por caja, o 4 Clamshells de 4Lb por caja
  
* Caja de 8.2 Kg - Incluye 9 ú 8 bolsas por caja, o 6 Clamshells de 3Lb por caja
  
* Caja de 9.1 Kg - Incluye 4 Clamshells de 5Lb por caja
  


  
 [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_comercial_01.jpg)](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#fd8e988f9a9492d38f9c898992bd909c9388989194899cd39e9290d38d98c68e9c91988ebd909c9388989194899cd39e9290d38d98c2ae889f97989e89c0be9293899c9e8992d8cfcd9e9290988f9e949c91d8cfcda88b9c)   [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_certificaciones_01.jpg)](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2017/06/Certificacion_Frutas_01.png)


La sugraone es una variedad de uva de mesa, sin semilla, de bayas de color verde claro. Su sabor es lígero y dulce, de textura crujiente.  
   
 La uva sugraone se cosecha y comercializa desde la primera semana de noviembre hasta mediados de diciembre.  
   
 Comercializamos la variedad Sugraone a mercados en Europa y Estados Unidos, con nuestras marcas **Manuelita e Inka Grapes**.  
   
 ![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Uvamesa_04.jpg)  
   
 **Disponibles en presentaciones:**  


  
 [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_comercial_01.jpg)](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#0e7d6b7c696761207c6f7a7a614e636f607b6b62677a6f206d6163207e6b357d6f626b7d4e636f607b6b62677a6f206d6163207e6b315d7b6c646b6d7a334d61607a6f6d7a612b3c3e6d61636b7c6d676f622b3c3e5b786f)   [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_certificaciones_01.jpg)](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2017/06/Certificacion_Frutas_01.png)


La Red Globe es una variedad de uva de mesa, con semilla, de bayas grandes de color rojo intenso y muy jugosas. Su sabor es afrutado.  
   
 La uva Red Globe se cosecha y comercializa desde mediados de diciembre hasta finales de enero.  
   
 Comercializamos la variedad Red Globe a mercados en Europa y Estados Unidos, con nuestras marcas **Manuelita e Inka Grapes**. Adicionalmente, llegamos a mercados en China con las marcas **Ohlinda, Yolin, Daria y Bonita.**  
   
 ![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Uvamesa_05.jpg)  
   
 **Disponibles en presentaciones:**  


  
 [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_comercial_01.jpg)](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#07746275606e68297566737368476a666972626b6e73662964686a2977623c74666b6274476a666972626b6e73662964686a297762385472656d6264733a446869736664736822353764686a6275646e666b223537527166)   [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_certificaciones_01.jpg)](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2017/06/Certificacion_Frutas_01.png)


La Crimson es una variedad de uva de mesa, sin semilla, de bayas alargadas de color rojo, piel firme y crujiente y pulpa jugosa. Su sabor es dulce, es baja en calorías y rica en antioxidantes y vitamina C. Se pueden consumir frescas como un aperitivo saludable o en ensaladas de frutas y platos de queso.  
   
 La uva Crimson se cosecha y comercializa desde la primera semana de diciembre hasta finales de febrero.  
   
 Comercializamos la variedad Crimson a mercados en Europa y Estados Unidos, con nuestras marcas **Manuelita e Inka Grapes**.  
   
 ![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Uvamesa_04.jpg)  
   
 **Disponibles en presentaciones:**  


  
 [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_comercial_01.jpg)](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#a4d7c1d6c3cdcb8ad6c5d0d0cbe4c9c5cad1c1c8cdd0c58ac7cbc98ad4c19fd7c5c8c1d7e4c9c5cad1c1c8cdd0c58ac7cbc98ad4c19bf7d1c6cec1c7d099e7cbcad0c5c7d0cb819694c7cbc9c1d6c7cdc5c8819694f1d2c5)   [![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Boton_certificaciones_01.jpg)](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2017/06/Certificacion_Frutas_01.png)

2Our seventh 
report presents 
Manuelita’s results in 
the economic, social, environmental and corporate governance dimensions, through 16 sustainability priorities, for the period between January 1, 2023, and December 31, 2024.It was prepared in 
collaboration with Corporate 
Human Resources and 
Sustainability and 
Manuelita’s business units, 
and was approved by the 
Organization’s CEO.
The indicators consolidate 
data from all business 
units  (unless speciﬁed 
otherwise with a footnote).this ReportAbout
diego.salazar@manuelita.com
monica.rengifo@manuelita.com
For more information on the content of this 
document, please contact Corporate Human Resources and Sustainability:


Our seventh Sustainability 
report highlights the company’s most important achievements in economic, social, environmental sustainability and good corporate governance between 2023 and 2024. It reﬂects our progress on 16 sustainability priorities and reaﬃrms our commitment to creating economic and social value, strengthening our initiatives for caring for the planet.from the CEOLetter
In the company’s sugar cane platform, 
Manuelita Azúc ar y Ener gía, optimized 
its extraction and energy eﬃciency 
processes at the factory level and made 
progress with implementing new irrigation 
technologies in the ﬁeld to reduce water 
consumption and improve productivity.It also renewed its Bonsucro 
certiﬁcation for sustainable production.
Agroindustrial Laredo increased 
crop productivity by implementing 
drip fertilization and irrigation 
systems, increased production of 
organic sugar, and implemented 
initiatives to improve extraction and 
energy eﬃciency at the factory level.
In the palm oil platform, Manuelit a 
Aceite y Energía, increased its 
production and sale of biodiesel and 
other value-added  products like 
reﬁned oil and glycerin. Palmar de 
Altamira, in turn, began 
incorporating new harvesting 
technologies, increased the 
reliability of its extraction process, 
and improved its work and 
accommodation facilities.
In the fruit and vegetable platform, 
Manuelit a Frutas y Hortaliz as 
continued its crop renewal plan with 
varieties of grapes that oﬀer higher 
productivity and market acceptance;  
and successfully harvested its ﬁrst 
blueberry crop in the north of Peru.
In the Aquaculture platform, 
Manuelita A cuicultura achieved its 
highest production and value-added 
sales levels for mussels, based on 
investments made in its processing and packing plant.
At the end of 2024, Manuelita reached 
an agreement with its partner Empagran in Ecuador, to sell Manuelita’s shares in Océanos, to better focus its resources on this and other agro-industrial platforms.
Our peopleAs part of our human talent 
management, we measured ourworkplace environment with Great Place to Work (GPTW) and signiﬁcantly reduced our accident rates, for the beneﬁt of our employees.
3
Photo: Bernardo Peña / Semana


Haro ld Eder
CEO
4In our social management with our 
lower income employees and neighboring communities, between 2023 and 2024 we helped graduate 774 individuals from high school; aﬃliated 2,618 people to the social security system in partnership with the public sector and provided 846 families with access to quality housing.
Regarding the generation of 
complementary income for the families of lower income employees, communities of inﬂuence and small agricultural suppliers, we supported an average of 117 family projects per year, which allowed them to improve their family incomes.
Fundación Manuelita, through its 
Educar Uno a Uno program, beneﬁted 1207 students, driving considerable improvement in the Saber 11 exams (national high school exit exams) at the Antonio Lizarazo Education Institution, that moved up from category A in 2023 to category A+ in 2024. The foundation also expanded the Educar Uno a Uno program to the Paulo VI school, located in the ﬁfth commune of Palmira.
Manuelita continue supporting 
Compromiso Valle, a program that helps close socio-economic gaps for 77,000 vulnerable young people in 8 municipalities in the department of Valle del Cauca, namely Cali, Buenaventura, Palmira, Yumbo, Buga, Jamundí, Candelaria and Tuluá. Between 2023 and 2024, the program impacted 608 young people in Palmira with leadership and employability training. As part of our job creation initiatives for vulnerable populations, 162 people were oﬀered jobs with Manuelita Azúcar y Energía.
Environmental ManagementRegarding environmental 
management, Manuelita reduced its Scope 1 & 2 emissions for in its sugar cane and palm oil platforms by 6% compared to 2023, making progress with its goal of reducing its Scope 1 & 2 carbon emissions by 70% by 2030 and achieving carbon neutrality by 2040.
Manuelita Azúcar y Energía made 
progress with establishing biological corridors, planting 8,000 trees of species native to Valle del Cauca along 12 km of forest belts in the ﬂatlands of the Amaime river basin.
Manuelita Azúcar y Energía and 
Manuelita Aceites y Energía achieved certiﬁcation of 360 thousand carbon credits resulting from greenhouse gas reductions, biomass energy generation, methane gas sequestration, and other circular economy initiatives.
Manuelita was recognized by the 
National Association of Businesspeople (Asociación Nacional de Empresarios – ANDI) and the National Trade Federation (Federación Nacional de Comerciantes - Fenalco) on its 160th anniversary and its contributions to Colombia’s social and economic development. It also ranked ﬁrst in the Agro-Industry category of the MERCO ranking of companies with the best corporate reputation in Colombia, and 57 on the general ranking.
On the 160th anniversary of our 
Organization, we are committed to our core purpose of generating progress and wellbeing through the sustainable use of natural resources for this and future generations.

Manuelita began operations 
in Colombia in 1864, with sugar cane crops and sugar production. In 1986 it began diversifying into other agro-industrial platforms including palm oil, aquaculture, fruits and vegetables.
With a 160-year history, 
Manuelita has a direct presence in Colombia, Peru and Chile, and exports its products to 65 countries around the world.

2023 - 2024Milestones
In 2024, Manuelita 
celebrated 160 years generating progress and wellbeing in Colombia.
10Its history goes back to 
1864 when its founder, Santiago Eder, purchased the La Manuelita and La Rita farms in Palmira, Valle del Cauca. These farms previously belonged to the father of the writer Jorge Isaacs, famous for his novel “La María”.
Today, 160 years 
later, Manuelita continues to work with conscience to harvest the fruit of a better tomorrow.
You are also a part of this history and of the future we will continue to build together.
Click here




In 2023 it implemented new 
technology in ﬁeld machinery and equipment to optimize mechanical planting processes in plots undergoing renovation.
Between 2023 and 2024 it 
implemented new irrigation systems, including pivot and drip systems, that reduce water consumption by up to 60% compared to conventional systems.
In 2023, it inaugurated the Aula 
Dulce Manuelita in partnership with Corporación Organización Minuto de Dios in the Sabio Mutis Agricultural Park located in Tena, Cundinamarca.In 2024, it certiﬁed 170,308 carbon credits, issued for the 2018-2021 period, for its CO2 emissions reduction initiatives.
In 2024, it launched its product 
line in paper packaging, which is more environmentally friendly, and its new products Golden and Natural White Sugar.
In 2024, it launched the Manuelita 
Innova program, which invites startups to submit innovative solutions to transform its commercial and logistical processes.
11In 2023, it commissioned a new crusher at its factory to improve 
sucrose extraction and reduce bagasse moisture content.
In 2024, it improved ﬁltration 
processes at the Arena Dulce ﬁelds, increasing water quality and optimizing drip irrigation for its plantations in that area.
In 2024 it achieved 
organic-vegan sugar certiﬁcation for the ﬁrst time.Manuelita
Azúcar y Energía:Agroindustrial
Laredo: 


In 2023 it certiﬁed 
189,301 carbon credits granted for the 2018-2021 period, for its circular economy initiatives that contribute to reducing CO2 emissions.
In 2023 it recertiﬁed 
its reﬁned glycerin production with HACCP , which 
ensures production safety.Manuelita
Aceites y
Energía:
In 2024 it renewed 
its RSPO certiﬁcation that accredits sustainable production.
In 2024 it 
commissioned a solar farm to increase energy self-suﬃciency, using renewable energy in common areas.Palmar de
Altamira:
In 2023 it began planting 32 hectares of blueberries and in 2024 it made its ﬁrst harvest, with a total production of 343 tons for sale.Manuelita

to the new Clean Production Agreement promoted by the government to achieve sustainability in the mussel farming sector.Manuelita

Manuelita is a diversiﬁed agroindustrial organization 
that operates mainly in the food and renewable energy sectors, with a presence in several countries and productive areas of the Americas.
It creates economic, social and environmental 
value through the eﬀective management of a portfolio of companies that deliver diﬀerentiated, high quality value-added products at a competitive total cost, made from renewable sources, through the sustainable use of natural resources, with a positive impact on the wellbeing of its neighboring communities.
Its target customers are found in local and 
international industrial and consumer channels.PropositionValue
14


In 2023, Coca-Cola FEMSA Colombia 
highlighted Manuelita as its supplier for the year in the Operational Performance category, due to its compliance, quality, safety, service, sustainability and transparency.
In 2024, Manuelita was recognized 
by the National Association of Businesses (ANDI) and the National Federation of Retailers (Fenalco) for its 160 years of history and contributions to Colombia’s social and economic developmentRecognition
MERCO Empresas, which measures the 100 companies with the best corporate reputation, ranked Manuelta at number 57 in 2024,and has placed it ﬁrst in the Agroindustry category overthe last eleven years.
Merco ESG, which measures corporate environmental responsibility, social responsibility and corporate governance ranked Manuelita at number 44 in the overall ranking in 2024, and, for nine consecutive years, in the ﬁrst place in the Agroindustry category.
In the category Care of the Environment, Manuelita 
moved up 8 positions compared to 2023, ranking 24th among top 100 companies.MERCO Talento, which appraises the best companies for talent attraction and retention in Colombia, ranked Manuelita number 53 overall for 2024, and number one in the Agroindustry sector.
Corporate ReputationPerformance
and Trajectory
15


Economic and
Social Development
In 2023, Agroindustrial Laredo 
received the “Companies that are Transforming Peru” award for its “Planting Progress” program, which aims to improve the productivity and standards of living of small sugarcane growers.
In 2024, Agroindustrial Laredo 
received an award from the regional government of La Libertad and the Regional Labor and Employment Promotion Committee for its good workplace inclusion practices.
In 2024, Manuelita Aceites y 
Energía received an award from the Manos Pintadas de Azul (Hands Painted Blue) Foundation for its commitment to the health and wellbeing of the communities that inhabit rural areas of San Carlos de Guaroa and Acacias in the department of Meta (Colombia).
16


Manuelita has deﬁned 16 
priorities through a sustainability model that sets the framework for its corporate and competitive strategy of its business units on economic, social, environmental, and good governance topics.
These priorities also state its 
commitment to help close gaps in the 2030 Sustainable Development Goals deﬁned by the United Nations.Sustainability

Manuelita’s values govern our 
engagement with all our stakeholders in all the countries where we operate.
“We live out our integrity 
every day, being consistent with our words and actions, even when we are not being watched. This is a fundamental value that reﬂects genuine respect for ourselves and for others.”
Lorena Chaves,  Corporate Audit Manager
21CultureValues-Centered 1.
Integrity
“We have reward 
programs that highlight outstanding performance. Our training model also aims to contribute to build competencies amongst our employees. ”Respect for people and
commitment to their development
Proper treatment, equality and 
recognition of the rights of our people are what govern human relations at Manuelita. We value diversity and we always seek to provide development opportunities for our employees, contributing to the organization’s goals.
Ana Gonzales, Human Resources Manager Manuelita AcuiculturaThis is at the core of all our values. It describes our 
actions and supports all our decisions. Ethics, honor, honesty, integrity, seriousness in business, a dedication to truth, are a centenary inheritance and those of us that work at Manuelita share these values. We respect the laws and rules of the countries, the governments, and the organizations we engage with.


22“At Manuelita we live out 
our social and 
environmental responsibility by promoting the wellbeing of our communities and caring for the environment, ensuring that our actions and products have a positive and sustainable impact on society.”Social and environmental
responsibility
We aim to make our corporate activities and our 
products sustainable, via a positive impact on the environment and the wellbeing of our neighboring communities, employees, clients and suppliers. We have a permanent commitment to and participation in improving the society where we live and work.
“At Manuelita we live out our 
Pioneering Spirit by exploring, thinking diﬀerent, reinventing ourselves and being creative, to move out of our safe zone. This requires a lot of discipline and enhancement of our capability for analyzing all the variables in play.”Pioneering spirit
We constantly seek out the best technology possible for the organization. In the businesses where we participate, we incorporate what we have learnt from the best and deﬁne a roadmap for innovation in the sector.
 Lidis Vanesa Herrera,  Environmental Specialist
 Carlos Mauricio Arias,  CEO of Manuelita Frutas y Hortalizas


23“Austerity at Manuelita means 
using our resources on what is 
really necessary: managing our operations’ environmental sustainability, generating progress and wellbeing amongst our employees and communities of inﬂuence, and maintaining long-term competitive business models in domestic and international markets.”Austerity
At Manuelita, austerity means investing in the essentials, with criteria of excellence, and without ostentation. We evaluate the contribution made by each investment and each expense, to ensure the continuity of our organization.
“Customer orientation guides 
our daily strategy and operations, as we include their needs at the core of our decisions. We build solid, long-term relations based on honesty, integrity, and a shared vision of value creation, which transcends beyond product delivery.”Customer orientation
We focus on developing long-term relationships with our clients, always delivering high-quality products and services. We aim to be preferred by our customers, based on an ongoing understanding of their needs and permanent eﬀorts to surpass their expectations.
Bernardo Atehortúa, CEO Palmar de Altamira
 Juan Felipe Forero, CSO Industria Manuelita Azúcar y Energía

The highest governance 
body is the General Shareholders Assembly of Inversiones Manuelita S.A., the parent companyof the corporategroup.The governance structure is conﬁgured by a Board of Directors, three committees, the CEOs of the business units and the corporate management oﬃces, which have been delegated responsibilities to manage economic, social and environmental issues.General

Henry Eder Caicedo
Lukas Garcés ArangoSandra Giovanelli EderJuan Antonio Zambrano EderRicardo SalaJorge Ramírez VallejoPatrick LoboCésar ZamoranoFrancisco Piedrahita PlataSantiago Eder GarcésMaría José Garcés SalmoiraghiMario Enrico Giovanelli EderJosé Manuel Zambrano EderJuan Miguel JaramilloJuan Carlos Morales ArangoSuzanne Lobo MaguireEnrico DucrotDiego Salazar
Principals AlternatesThe Board of Directors is composed of 
nine principal members, with their corresponding alternates. Three principal members are external, non-shareholders.
Their purpose is to guide the Organization’s strategy and corporate management, as well as other functions deﬁned in the Good Governance Code.
They have a term of two years.Board members receive a ﬁxed 
compensation fee determined by the General Shareholders Assembly.
The Chair of the Board of Directors does 
not hold an executive position within the Organization.
Manuelita’s Executive CEO has a voice but 
not a vote on the board.Board of Directors
26


Conﬁgured by six principal 
members of the Board of 
Directors. It deﬁnes policies and guidelines for each business unit in the following areas:
Selection and hiring

Directors. It analyzes cases related to conﬂicts of interest of members of the Board of Directors and employees, regulated in the Manuelita Code of Conduct.

analyzes other highly relevant cases reported through the Ethics Line

At the end of 
2024, all cases reported to the Ethics Line have been closed.Reports received via the Ethics Line are rigorously analyzed to identify opportunities for improvement and deﬁne corrective or preventive measures to reinforce an ethical culture.

ensuring proper information processing and decision making in line with the organization’s values.1.

Manuelita Azúcar y 
Energía, Manuelita Aceites y Energía, and Agroindustrial Laredo have Kosher certiﬁcation, a seal of approval issued by rabbinic agencies that verify that the food we produce, including sugar, oil and derivatives, meet Jewish dietary laws.Kosher
Manuelita Azúcar y Energía and Agroindustrial Laredo have Halal certiﬁcation, 
which provides assurance that our products comply with Muslim dietary laws for product preparation and storage. This certiﬁcation opens doors to export to Muslim countries and to oﬀer our products to practitioners of Islam.Halal
These standards make sure our products meet quality, food safety and environmental and social responsibility criteria. They also allow us to provide our clients and consumers with conﬁdence and security in our products.Manuelita Azúcar and Energy has the BONSUCRO seal that certiﬁes that sugar cane production meets high environmental and social sustainability standards in its supply chain and at 54% of its plantations (company and supplier owned).Bonsucro
51
Sustainability
Certiﬁcations


Manuelita Aceites y Energía and 
Palmar de Altamira have this international certiﬁcation that guarantees sustainable manufacturing of all products produced by its production plant and its own crops, including crude palm oil (CPO), crude palm kernel oil (CPKO) and other subproducts derived from the oil palm tree, in its operations located in the departments of Casanare and Meta, respectively.RSPO (Roundtable on
Sustainable Palm Oil)
Manuelita Aceites y Energía has 
been certiﬁed under the ISCC standard, that certiﬁes sustainable production in its own palm oil crops and its biodiesel processing plant.ISCC (International
Sustainability & CarbonCertiﬁcation) 
52N° 1-0163-14-100-00




Manuelita Aceites y Energía and Manuelita 
Frutas y Hortalizas have HACCP certiﬁcation which allows the companies that produce food grade products to prove their commitment to the safe production of raw materials and foodstuﬀs.HACCP
Manuelita Frutas y Hortalizas has the SMETA certiﬁcation, which 
certiﬁes compliance with work, health and safety, environmental and ethical standards within its operations.SMETA
Manuelita Aquaculture is certiﬁed by the ASC (Aquaculture Stewardship Council) that accredits its social and environmental commitment within its sea aquaculture projects.ASC

57We generate 
electricity from renewable sources that allow us to cover our production requirements in Colombia and Peru. We use biomass including sugar cane bagasse, palm ﬁber, husks and rachis, which are byproducts of our industrial processes.
In our Meta operations, we produce energy from biogas, capturing and 
utilizing the methane generated by runoﬀ from palm oil extraction.
We also generate surplus bioelectricy for residential use through allies 
that commercialize it.

As part of our focus on 
providing clean energy from renewable sources, we produce and sell biofuel produced from sugar cane and palm oil.
Manuelita Azúcar y Energía produces automotive bioethanol, sold according to government-approved mixture percentages for fossil fuel oxygenation.Biofuels are one of the main solutions for reducing greenhouse gas emissions, energy diversiﬁcation in each country, and improving air quality.

We oﬀer value-added 
palm oil subproducts, including reﬁned glycerin, an ecological and sustainable alterative to animal sourced or synthetic glycerin.Thanks to its humidifying, emollient and solvent properties, it is used for manufacturing pharmaceutical, cosmetic, and personal hygiene and care products.

At Manuelita we also 
develop innovative and sustainable solutions to strengthen Colombia’s agriculture. Our BIORGANIK and BIOFERTI-K products, 
manufactured from renewable sources, oﬀer multiple beneﬁts for soils and crops. They improve fertility, plant nutrition and health, and drive crop productivity and quality.
We are supported by farmers who have proven their eﬀectiveness for sugar-cane, palm oil, rice, coﬀee, pineapple, plantain, avocado, and citrus crops, among others. 4.5236.907Biofertilizers

Manuelita has decided to break the 
gender paradigms inherent to an agroindustry operation that is made up of positions historically performed by men.
This process began in 2023 with 
the following actions:
Identiﬁcation of positions historically occupied by men.
Attraction of women to promote their 
participation in selection processes.
Adaptation and facility improvements 
in ﬁeld bathrooms and common areas to close gaps between men and women.


At Manuelita we are committed to our 
employees’ development.
Our Performance Management System (PMS) 
allows us to measure business target fulﬁllment and 
the individual objectives deﬁned for each employee. 
We also identify potential competency gaps and 
monitor development actions for improvement. 
Performance monitoring promotes spaces for 
feedback, with two-way conversations between 
bosses and employees.

Manuelita has a training model 
that includes job training 
processes and courses for our 
administrative and operational 
employees, in three areas: 
Leadership development, includes

that aims to develop competent and committed leaders under a values-based organizational culture and organizational competencies, contributing to the construction of a Manuelita leadership brand.

leaders through corporate knowledge management.Speciﬁc objectives

and loyalty of our human talent, while promoting social

assistance in product design, portfolio development and 
digital marketing for 45 startups and 51 productive units in the commercial, manufacturing, service and food sectors.
In 2023, it supported WERO, an initiative for inclusive 
businesses that focuses on creating jobs for persons living in vulnerable conditions. In 2024 WERO was consolidated as an apparel supplier for Manuelita Azúcar y Energía.Manuelita Azúcar y Energía
Between 2023 and 2024, an average of COP $888M in complementary income were generated per year, beneﬁting 66 families with an average additional monthly income of COP$1,121,329 per family.

90The “An Entrepreneur Like You” program continues to 
promote the generation of additional income for families of employees, communities and small sugar cane suppliers, through training, technical assistance and consulting to strengthen their startups in the areas of catering, textile manufacturing, and cosmetology.Agroindustrial Laredo
Between 2023 and 2024, this generated an average of S/.411 thousand per year in complementary income, beneﬁting 37 families with an average additional monthly income of S/. 926 per family.
Continued promoting the Mujeres El Triunfo women’s initiative, a startup created by the wives of employees and women from the community, who design and manufacture industrial uniforms for Manuelita Aceites y Energía. Local startups are also developing as the main catering services supplying refreshments and wellbeing activities.Manuelita Aceites y Energía
Between 2023 and 2024, this program generated COP $205M in complementary income, beneﬁting 14 families with an average monthly additional income of COP $1,263,421 per family.

Manuelita and El Minuto de Dios 
strengthen their partnership by 
inaugurating the Aula Dulce 
Manuelita
To strengthen its joint 
commitment to improve the country’s social wellbeing and education, Manuelita and El Minuto de Dios inaugurated Aula Dulce Manuelita (Manuelita Sweet Classroom) in 2023.This playful, recreational space can be 
found in the Sabio Mutis Agricultural Park, which belongs to Corporación Universitaria Minuto de Dios UNIMINUTO.
Manuelita transformed this space to teach 
visitors to the Agricultural Park about the history of sugarcane, sugar, and the process for making panela, a traditional product from the region. This space also tells the history of Manuelita and the contribution made by its founder, Santiago Eder, to the country’s industrial development.
See more
95


96Growing Talent
To break cycles of poverty with quality education and to provide more 
opportunities and development for the region, Manuelita started its “Cultivando Talentos” (Growing Talent) scholarship program. This initiative provides 21 young people, the children of lower income employees of Manuelita 
Azúcar y Energía and students that beneﬁt from the Educar Uno a Uno program of the Manuelita foundation, with the possibility to make their dream of accessing higher education come true.active scholarship recipients, 7 of them from the Manuelita Foundation and 9 children of employees.
16At the end of 2024, the program has
Scholarship for 90% of tuition fees for each academic program through graduation.




Fundación Manuelita
It beneﬁts the Antonio Lizarazo 
educational institution, at its Rosa Zárate and Central facilities, located in the 1st Commune of Palmira; and the Paulo VI school at its Central installations, located in the 5th Commune of Palmira. These territories are home to displaced populations and have high levels of violence and poverty. 
Educar Uno a Uno implements its life 
transformation strategy through three components:
Since 2014, Fundación 
Manuelita has implemented its Educar Uno a Uno (Educate One by One) program. This is a comprehensive model that aims to transform the lives of students and their families, teachers and school directors, through education. 1.207

Technology 
In 2015, Manuelita Azúcar y Energía implemented its 
irrigation master plan, which adopts the best technology for ﬁeld irrigation, including buried pipe and drip and spray irrigation systems, that improve water use eﬃciency on its own and on third party crop areas.
use the buried pipe water conduction system, that reduces water losses. At the end of 2024,Sugar cane
Agroindustrial Laredo is recognized for its pioneering project that turned desert areas into soil suitable for sugar cane production, where it uses advanced drip irrigation and fertilization technology on

112Fruit and Vegetables
Blueberry crop irrigation is highly automated. 
The drip irrigation system manages the amount of water and nutrients required by each plant and is automatically calibrated to adjust to the optimum conditions that the crop requires.
This 
reduces water consumption by up to 
50%  compared to conventional methods. Manuelita Frutas y Hortalizas uses a mechanized drip irrigation system on
of table grapes, blueberries, asparagus and citrus fruits.hectares618

Advances 2023-2024:Manuelita Azúcar y Energía participates in the Water for 
Life and Sustainability Fund, a social and environmental initiative implemented by the sugar cane agroindustry in cooperation with public and private actors.
With this program, it supports conservation and restoration 
eﬀorts in tributary basins of the Cauca River.
Intervened 886 hectares and 26 basins in 38 municipalities.
Established 45% of hectares (397.6 hectares) as isolation areas for ecosystem protection and recovery.Implemented over 162 food security modules to beneﬁt over

Since 2021, Manuelita adopted the ISO 
14046 water footprint methodology for its Sugar Cane and Palm platforms, which considers:
Scope:
1
Water volume removed and/or 
extracted, required for production processes.
2
Water consumption associated with electrical energy purchases.
3
Water consumption in the supply chain, required to produce the required goods (raw materials and inputs) and services, including transportation to the productive process.


119Scope 1 & 2 water footprint reports the freshwater 
consumption required by ﬁeld and factory operations and indirect water consumption associated with electrical energy purchased in 2023 and 2024.

Carbon Footprint
Our goal is to reduce greenhouse gas and 
particulate matter emissions by using clean technologies in our production plants, increasing natural energy sources and reducing agricultural  biomass burning for harvesting.
Manuelita calculated its carbon footprint for its sugar 
cane and palm oil platforms under the GHG Protocol and ISO 14067:2018 international standards, to measure, control and contribute to climate change mitigation.
Emissions classiﬁed by scope:
125
1Direct emissions
generated by operations. 
2
Indirect emissions generated by electrical energy purchases.
3
Indirect emissions related to purchased goods (raw materials and inputs).


The organization’s Scope 1 & 2 carbon footprint 
reﬂects direct emissions generated by its ﬁeld and factory operations and its indirect emissions associated with electrical energy purchases, respectively, during 2023 and 2024:

2023 2024266.706
251.2384.633271.339 256.205
4.967Scope 1
 Scope 2
Manuelita reduced its Scope 1 
& 2 carbon footprint by 6% between 2023 and 2024, due to increased use of renewable energy to replace fossil fuels like coal and natural gas.
In 2024, Manuelita committed to a 70% reduction in its Scope 1 & 2 emissions by 2030, intending to make its Palm Oil and Sugar Cane platforms carbon neutral by 2040.

Green harvesting (Colombia and Peru)
In its sugar cane platform, Manuelita has committed to 
reducing its greenhouse gas emissions in the ﬁeld, increasing the number of green hectares harvested (without biomass burning for harvesting purposes).
Since 2021, Manuelita Azucar y Energía green harvests 
100% of its crops. Any gaps are due to accidental ﬁres that occurred during dry spells.
Agroindustrial Laredo continues complying with the 
Environmental Adaptation and Management Plan (“PAMA”, in Spanish) that it implemented in 2015, reducing burning for harvesting on 1,933 hectares between 2023 and 2024 in ﬁelds nearby urban areas.

and PromotionBiodiversity Protection14.
Biological Corridors
High Value Conservation AreasReforestation ProgramsManuelita at the COP-16 


We are committed to environmental 
sustainability and biodiversity conservation in all our operations.Biological
Corridors
133Manuelita Azúcar y Energía protects biological corridors 
and watersheds in Valle del Cauca through ecosystem reforestation, maintenance and protection activities.
In 2023 and 2024 it planted 2,695 trees in nearby river 
basins, for a total of 7,862 trees planted along 14.3 protected kilometers.Sugar caneManuelita Azúcar y Energía received a grant from the 
Bonsucro Impact Fund to restore natural ecosystems and improve the ﬂow of native species in Valle del Cauca.
This is an inter-institutional coordination and 
cooperation project in partnership with Cenicaña, RG y CIA, Asoamaime and Asobolo.It has four main purposes:
Establish ecological connectivity corridors to foster biodiversity ﬂows.
Build and strengthen nurseries 
to propagate and recover native species in the Amaime and Bolo river basins.Implement a water monitoring network.
 
Eﬃcient water use for irrigation.


In its Palm Oil operations in Meta and Casanare, 
Manuelita preserves and protects a total of 1,256 hectares known as High Value Conservation Areas (HVCs), where extraction activities, crops, hunting and ﬁshing are forbidden.
In 2024, Manuelita Aceites y Energía carried out sensitization events for its employees and communities in its area of inﬂuence, and began monitoring rare, threatened or endangered species in the 848 hectares of HVCs that it protects.
In turn, Palmar de Altamira implements 
a conservation plan for 407.9 hectares of HVCs, promoting plant and soil recovery and sensitizing employees and communities nearby the plantation.Palm oil
134High Conservation
Value Areas 


135Reforestation
Programs  
In 2023, Manuelita Azúcar y 
Energía inaugurated the La Rita 
Forestry Nursery, with a production 
capacity of 38,000 native seedlings 
for tropical dry forest reforestation 
activities. In 2024 it carried out 
seed collection and germination 
events, producing 23,000 
seedlings.
In 2023, Agroindustrial Laredo 
implemented a reforestation program to strengthen biodiversity protection at its crop areas, planting 1,430 trees in the Laredo 
and Arena Dulce areas.
In 2024, it planted 540 trees 
throughout diﬀerent crop areas.Sugar cane Palm oil
As of 2024, Manuelita 
Aceites y Energía has planted 65,780 plants on 59.8 hectares set aside for reforestation.  
In turn, in 2024, Palmar de 
Altamira planted 2,689 trees on 136.6 reforestation hectares that border onto High Value Conservation Areas.
See video


Within the framework of COP-16, held in Cali, 
Colombia, in October 2024, Manuelita shared its progress in environmentally sustainable agroindustry, focusing on circular economy and biodiversity integration within its business model.This initiative was an opportunity to interact with the community and share the community gardens project.Participation on 4 panels aimed at diﬀerentstakeholder groups.
Participation with a stand in the green zone that oﬀered 
experiential workshops in urban and residential gardens, in cooperation with Universidad ICESI.
136Manuelita
at COP-16
See more


137
careSoil 15.
Compost and organic residue
production and application


138We use organic fertilizers and irrigation systems 
that protect the soil.
We have also adopt ed technologies to prevent 
soil compacting, which limits air and water 
circulation and reduces crop yields.Compost and Organic
Residue Production
and Application
Manuelita Azúcar y Energía and Agroindustrial 
Laredo produce compost from the organic 
residue of its production processes.Sugar cane
Palm oilBetween 2023 and 2024, Manuelita Azúcar y 
Energía applied compost to 1,250 hectares of organic sugar cane.
Between 2023 and 2024, Agroindustrial 
Laredo increased the organic matter content 
of desert soils and its organic ﬁelds, applying compost to 1,373 hectares.
At the close of 2024, Manuelita Aceites y Energía had applied compost to 1,300 hectares of crops, replacing chemical fertilizers on 270.7 hectares.
Palmar de Altamira applied rachis, a 
subproduct of palm oil extraction, to fertilize 796 hectares of crops.See video


Circular
Economy 
16.
Residue Transformation
Carbon CreditsUse and Final Disposaland Residue Management
139


At Manuelita we use the residues from 
our agroindustry processes in our circular economy processes.Residue
Transformation
140Biomass for energy production
Sugar cane bagasse and palm ﬁber, husks, 
and rachis, constitute biomass obtained from sugar cane milling and palm oil extraction. We use this to produce electrical energy.
Biomass oﬀers several environmental 
advantages, including greenhouse gas emission reductions and lowerdependence on fossil fuels.
Industrial eﬄuents for fertilization 
and irrigation
We use industrial eﬄuents generated by 
crude palm oil extraction to fertilize and irrigate our crop, eliminating wastewater runoﬀ into surface bodies of water and reducing the use of chemical fertilizers.


141Pellet production from bagasse
Agroindustrial Laredo produces pellets that 
are used for heating, energy production and animal feed. As a naturally sourced product, it is ecological and sustainable and replaces the use of fossil fuels like coal.Organic residue in compost
We produce compost from the organic residue of our productive processes, 
including cane sludge, ash, among others, to use for organic crop fertilization.
Bales for compost and renewableelectrical energy production
Agroindustrial Laredo compacts biomass into bales that are used to produce compost and generate renewable electrical energy.


142Each carbon credit granted is equivalent to one ton
of CO2 that is not emitted into the atmosphere.Committed to reducing the environmental impact of 
our operations, we generated value-added products using a circular economy approach.
Leveraged by that business model, we have produced 
carbon credits that we trade on voluntary emissions oﬀset markets.In 2024, Manuelita Azúcar y 
Energía certiﬁed 170,308 
carbon credits, granted for 
the 2018-2021 period, due to its CO2 emissions reduction initiatives.In 2023, Manuelita Aceites y Energía certiﬁed 189,301 
carbon credits, granted for 
the 2021-2022 period, due to its CO2 emissions reduction initiatives.Carbon Credits

## Prioridades de Sostenibilidad Económica
**Fuente original:** [https://www.manuelita.com/economico/](https://www.manuelita.com/economico/)


# Económico


Manuelita genera progreso y bienestar a partir de la gestión efectiva de un portafolio de empresas que entregan productos diferenciados con valor agregado e alta calidad a costo total competitivo.


![scroll](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/down-white.png)


  ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/2.png)


## Prioridades de Sostenibilidad Económica


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-21.png)


## Diversificación e internacionalización


Avances Plataformas Agroindustriales


* ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-57.png)


  ## Caña de Azúcar


  ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Icon-33.png)


  ## Unidades de negocio


  Manuelita Azúcar y Energía y Agroindustrial Laredo


  ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Icon-34.png)


  ## Comercializa


  • Azúcar  
   • Bioetanol carburante  
   • Bioetanol industrial  
   • Bioenergía eléctrica  
   • Créditos de carbono  
   • Otros subproductos a partir de la caña de azúcar (aditivos, fertilizantes orgánicos, otros)


  ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Icon-35.png)


  ## Sectores


  Alimentos, farmacéutico, energía y materiales de construcción
* ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-58.png)


  ## Palma de aceite


  Manuelita Aceites y Energía y Palmar de Altamira


  Alimentos, cosméticos y energía
* ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-59.png)


  ## Frutas y Hortalizas


  Manuelita Frutas y Hortalizas


  ## Mercados


  Europa, Asia y el continente americano
* ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-60.png)


  ## Acuicultura


  Manuelita Acuicultura


  Europa, Asia y el continente americano


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-22.png)


## Destinos de exportación


Manuelita mantiene vínculos comerciales con 65 países a través de sus operaciones, exportaciones e importaciones.


  ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Diseno-sin-titulo-2.png)


## Atención diferencial y de alta calidad


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-23.png)


## Satisfacción de clientes


Se llevó a cabo la evaluación de la percepción de los clientes industriales y distribuidores en Colombia y Perú mediante la encuesta realizada por el Centro Nacional de Consultoría. Los resultados indican que Manuelita es un proveedor confiable, de alto prestigio y cercano a sus clientes. Estos atributos refuerzan el posicionamiento de Manuelita como una de las marcas líderes en su sector.


  ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-61.png)


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-24.png)


## Certificaciones de calidad


En Manuelita ofrecemos alimentos y energías renovables, elaborados con altos estándares de calidad que garantizan la inocuidad alimentaria de nuestros productos y el cuidado del medio ambiente en nuestras operaciones.


Conoce las certificación de:


* Azúcar y Energía
* Agroindustrial Laredo
* Aceites y Energía


* Palmar de Altamira
* Frutas y Hortalizas ⁬⁭   ⁬⁭
* Acuicultura


[Ingresando ahora a nuestro informe de sostenibilidad ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/green.png)](#)


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-25.png)


## Sellos de sostenibilidad


Estos estándares aseguran que nuestros productos cumplen criterios de calidad, inocuidad y responsabilidad ambiental y social. Además, nos permiten ofrecer a nuestros clientes y consumidores confianza y seguridad en nuestros procesos.


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/ImageWithFallback-7.png)


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/ImageWithFallback-6.png)


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/ImageWithFallback-5.png)


[Descubre más en nuestras certificaciones en el informe de Sostenibilidad 2023 – 2024 ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/green.png)](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Informe-de-Sostenibilidad-Manuelita-2023-2024.pdf)


## Productos de valor agregado con menor impacto ambiental


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-26.png)


### Azúcar orgánica


Azúcar orgánica en Colombia y Perú:


Producimos azúcar orgánica certificada en Colombia y Perú, cultivada sin el uso de insumos químicos y respetando los ciclos naturales del suelo.


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-28.png)


### Bioenergía eléctrica


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-30.png)


### Biocombustibles


Como parte de nuestro enfoque de ofrecer energías limpias provenientes de fuentes renovables, producimos y comercializamos biocombustibles a partir de la caña de azúcar y palma de aceite.


• produce bioetanol carburante, que se comercializa de acuerdo con el porcentaje de mezclas aprobado por los gobiernos para oxigenar gasolina de origen fósil.  
 Manuelita Azúcar y Energía


• produce bioetanol industrial que se usa en la industria química y farmacéutica como disolvente para la fabricación de productos.  
 Agroindustrial Laredo


• produce biodiesel que se comercializa de acuerdo con el porcentaje de mezclas aprobado por los gobiernos para oxigenar de diesel de origen fósil.  
 Manuelita Aceites y Energía


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-31.png)


### Biofertilizantes


En Manuelita también desarrollamos soluciones innovadoras y sostenibles para fortalecer la agricultura en Colombia.


elaborados a partir de fuentes renovables, ofrecen múltiples beneficios para el suelo y los cultivos, como mejorar la fertilidad, nutrición y salud de las plantas, además de impulsar la productividad y calidad de las cosechas.


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-27.png)


### Azúcar en empaques de papel


 ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-9.png)


[Ver comercial del lanzamiento](#)


![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Container-29.png)


### Glicerina refinada


Ofrecemos subproductos de valor agregado derivados de la palma de aceite, como la glicerina refinada, una alternativa ecológica y sostenible frente a la glicerina de origen animal o sintético.


Gracias a sus propiedades humectantes, emolientes y solventes, es empleada en la fabricación de productos farmacéuticos, cosméticos y de cuidado e higiene personal.


 ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/GlicerinaChart.png)


[Descubre más en nuestro informe de Sostenibilidad 2023 – 2024 ![](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/blank.png)](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Informe-de-Sostenibilidad-Manuelita-2023-2024.pdf)

## Derivados de palma Agroindustrial

# Derivados de palma


Manuelita ofrece sub productos derivados de valor agregado, obtenidos a partir de la palma de aceite, que sirven como base para la fabricación de productos alimenticios, farmacéuticos, cosméticos y de cuidado personal principalmente.


![Aceite crudo de palma](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/aceite_crudo_de_palma.png)


Aceite crudo de palma ![Aceite crudo de palma](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


 ![Glicerina refinada](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Glicerina-Refinada.jpg)


Glicerina refinada ![Glicerina refinada](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


 ![Torta de palmiste](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Torta-De-palmiste.jpg)


Torta de palmiste ![Torta de palmiste](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


 ![Aceite de palmiste](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/aceiteDepalmiste.jpg)


Aceite de palmiste ![Aceite de palmiste](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_select.svg)


Manuelita produce aceite crudo de palma en los departamentos del Meta y Casanare en Colombia, a través de sus unidades de negocio:  
   
 Manuelita Aceites y Energía (Meta)  
 Palmar de Altamira (Casanare)  


  
* Ver lista de verificación plantas extractoras certificadas y no certificadas. [Ver aquí](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/2024_2nd-Half_Aceites-Manuelita-S.A_.xlsx)
  
* Ver documentos disponibles al público Palmar de Altamira. [Ver aquí](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Documentos-palmar-altamira.pdf)
  
* Ver documentos ControlUnion [Versión en Español](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/ReviewedRSPO-Public-Announcement-Template-APR-2021-LATAM-Palmar-de-Altamira-SPANISH.pdf) | [English version](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/ReviewedRSPO-Public-Announcement-Template-APR-2021-Palmar-de-Altamira-ENG.pdf)

[Certificaciones](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/FR-PP-EA-0027-LISTA-DE-VERIFICACIÓN-DE-PLANTAS-EXTRACTORAS-CERTIFICADAS-Y-NO-CERTIFICADAS.pdf)


  


[Documentos a su disposición en Derivados de la palma - Aceite crudo de palma](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Documentos-Manuelita.pdf)


Producimos glicerina refinada a partir de nuestros cultivos de aceite de palma, como una alternativa ecológica y sostenible frente a la glicerina de origen animal o sintético. Se utiliza como materia prima para la fabricación de productos farmacéuticos, cosméticos y de cuidado e higiene personal, gracias a propiedades humectantes, emolientes y solventes.  
   
 **Producido en Colombia por Manuelita Aceites y Energía**  
 ![](https://www.manuelita.com//wp-content/uploads/2016/10/manuelita_horizontal_01.png)  
   
 Producimos glicerina refinada a partir de nuestros cultivos de aceite de palma, como una alternativa ecológica y sostenible frente a la glicerina de origen animal o sintético. Se utiliza como materia prima para la fabricación de productos farmacéuticos, cosméticos y de cuidado e higiene personal, gracias a propiedades humectantes, emolientes y solventes.  
   
 **Cumple con estándares nacionales e internacionales:**  


  
* HACCP bajo la norma NTC 5830 (Requisitos para el análisis de peligros y puntos de control critico)
  
* KOSHER
  
* USP (United States Pharmacopeia)
  


[![](/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/comprar.svg) Quiero comprar Glicerina refinada](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#77161b121d161913051859141619161b1204371a161902121b1e03165914181a)


Por su alto contenido en grasas, proteínas y fibra, la torta de palmiste es utilizada en la elaboración de alimentos concentrados y purinas para animales, especialmente para el ganado bovino al ser una valiosa fuente de energía.  
   
 La torta de palmiste producida por Manuelita Aceites se obtiene a partir del proceso de extracción de aceite de palmiste por medio del prensado mecánico (expeller) de la almendra del fruto de palma.  
   


[![](/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/comprar.svg) Quiero comprar Torta de Palmiste](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#cdaca1a8a7aca3a9bfa2e3aeaca3aca1a8be8da0aca3b8a8a1a4b9ace3aea2a0f29eb8afa7a8aeb9f08ea2a3b9acaeb9a2e8fcfdaea2a0a8bfaea4aca1e8fffd99a2bfb9ace8fffd9daca1a0a4beb9a8)


[Certificaciones](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/06/Certificacion_Biodiesel_01.png)  
 La torta de palmiste de Manuelita está certificada bajo los estándares de sostenibilidad RSPO


El Aceite crudo de palmiste (CPKO) se utiliza en la industria oleo química para la elaboración de cosméticos, jabones y productos de limpieza, y productos farmacéuticos.  
   
 También se utiliza como materia prima en la elaboración de productos alimenticios como confitería y heladería (grasas para recubrimiento de aluminio, para la fabricación de sustitutos de manteca de cacao, grasas para confitería, masas para galletas, cremas para rellenos, glaseado para tortas, helados, imitación de crema batida, margarinas de fusión rápida y de mesa, aceites de cocina y de horneado).  
   
 Se obtiene por proceso de extracción mecánica (prensado) de la almendra de la palma de aceite.  
   
 En Manuelita Aceites y Energía es producida con base en los principios y criterios establecidos por la RSPO.  
   


[![](/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/comprar.svg) Quiero comprar aceite de Palmiste](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#6e0f020b040f000a1c01400d0f000f020b1d2e030f001b0b02071a0f400d0103513d1b0c040b0d1a532d01001a0f0d1a014b5c5e0d01030b1c0d070f024b5c5e2f0d0b071a0b4b5c5e3e0f0203071d1a0b) [Certificaciones](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/06/Certificacion_Biodiesel_01.png)

## Bioetanol con ventajas ambientales
**Fuente original:** [https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-productos/bioetanol/](https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-productos/bioetanol/)


[![Compartir](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_share.svg)](#)  [![Compartir en Facebook](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_facebook_white.svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?title=Bioetanol&u=https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-productos/bioetanol/)  [![Compartir en Twitter](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/logo_X.png)](http://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-productos/bioetanol/&text= Bioetanol)  [![Compartir en Linkedin](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon-linkedIn-4.svg)](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?url=https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-productos/bioetanol/)  [![Ir a Instagram](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/logo_ig.png)](https://www.instagram.com/manuelitaagroindustria/)


# Bioetanol


Manuelita produce y comercializa bioetanol con ventajas ambientales, como la reducción de las emisiones de gases de efecto invernadero y la disminución de la dependencia del petróleo:


## Bioetanol carburante:


Producimos y comercializamos bioetanol carburante a partir del procesamiento de caña de azúcar en el Valle del Cauca, en Colombia. El bioetanol es un combustible renovable que se obtiene a partir de la fermentación de azúcares procedentes de cultivos agrícolas o de residuos orgánicos. Se puede utilizar como carburante para motores de gasolina, ya sea puro o mezclado con éste.


## Bioetanol industrial:


Producimos y comercializamos bioetanol industrial a partir del procesamiento de caña de azúcar en Trujillo, Perú. El bioetanol industrial es un tipo de alcohol que se utiliza para fines no alimentarios, como la limpieza, la desinfección, el combustible o la síntesis de otras sustancias.

# Ruta Emprendedora Manuelita: un programa integral para impulsar el desarrollo de emprendedores en la región


![Ruta emprendedora manuelita](/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/foto-Ruta-Emprendedora-Manuelita_-un-programa-integral-para-impulsar-el-desarrollo-de-emprendedores-en-la-region.png)


Con más de 160 años de trayectoria generando progreso y bienestar, Manuelita presenta la Ruta Emprendedora Manuelita, un programa de acompañamiento diseñado para impulsar el desarrollo personal y empresarial de emprendedores de Palmira y su zona de influencia.


El programa está dirigido a emprendedores de Palmira, Amaime, Rozo, El Cerrito, Ginebra, Pradera, Candelaria y Florida, incluyendo colaboradores de Manuelita, sus familiares y miembros de las comunidades vecinas. La propuesta formativa está a cargo de Impacto E Company Builder, con 16 años de experiencia en desarrollo empresarial, y se lleva a cabo de manera 100% presencial en las instalaciones de Manuelita, sin costo para los participantes.


Con la Ruta Emprendedora, Manuelita reafirma su compromiso con la generación de valor compartido, articulando el crecimiento económico con la sostenibilidad social y ambiental de las comunidades donde opera.


[![Compartir en Facebook](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_facebook_white.svg)](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-noticias/ruta-emprendedora-manuelita-programa-integral-para-emprendedores/)  [![Compartir en Twitter](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/themes/manuelita/images/icons/icon_twitter_white.svg)](http://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.manuelita.com/manuelita-noticias/ruta-emprendedora-manuelita-programa-integral-para-emprendedores/&text= Ruta Emprendedora Manuelita: un programa integral para impulsar el desarrollo de emprendedores en la región)

# Únete a nuestro equipo


En Manuelita, estamos comprometidos con el desarrollo sostenible y la innovación. Valoramos a las personas apasionadas que quieren marcar una diferencia en la agroindustria y en el bienestar de nuestras comunidades. Si te motiva ser parte de una organización global con más de 160 años de historia, que fomenta el crecimiento profesional y personal, ¡esta es tu oportunidad!

# ¡Manuelita es la empresa #1 del sector agroindustrial en Responsabilidad ESG!


![¡Manuelita es la empresa #1 del sector agroindustrial en Responsabilidad ESG!](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/destacada-¡Manuelita-es-la-empresa-1-del-sector-agroindustrial-en-Responsabilidad-ESG.png)


Por 10 años consecutivos, somos la empresa número #1 del sector agroindustrial en el ranking Merco Responsabilidad ESG Colombia para el año 2025 (Medio Ambiente, Sociedad y Gobierno Corporativo), y posición #55 en el ranking general, por nuestro compromiso con el medio ambiente, la sociedad y el buen gobierno corporativo.


También ingresamos al top 50 en el ranking de las empresas más responsables con sus colaboradores, clientes y sociedad y alcanzamos la posición # 35 en el escalafón del cuidado del medio ambiente.


En Manuelita la sostenibilidad la hacemos todos. Por eso, valoramos, destacamos y agradecemos el compromiso de nuestros colaboradores en estos más de 160 años de historia. Juntos le apostamos a una agroindustria sostenible y responsable que entrega productos de valor agregado con menor impacto ambiental, y genera progreso y bienestar.


[Conoce más sobre nuestra estrategia de Sostenibilidad](https://www.manuelita.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Informe-de-Sostenibilidad-Manuelita-2023-2024.pdf)


[Conoce más sobre el ranking Merco Responsabilidad ESG Colombia 2025](https://www.merco.info/co/)
